designation: Maintainer & TSC Member at AsyncAPI (part of Linux Foundation) | Software Engineer at WatchGuard

projects.name: AsyncAPI Website
projects.short_description: The official website and documentation platform for AsyncAPI.
projects.description: The AsyncAPI website is the central platform for documentation, tooling, community resources, blogs, and contributor onboarding for AsyncAPI (part of Linux Foundation). The project focuses on improving developer experience, accessibility, contributor workflows, and documentation.

projects.name: AsyncAPI Parser JS
projects.description: Parser JS is one of the core AsyncAPI tooling projects that enables parsing, validating, and working with AsyncAPI specifications programmatically within JavaScript and Node.js ecosystems.

projects.name: AsyncAPI OpenAPI Schema Parser
projects.short_description: Parser for handling OpenAPI schemas within AsyncAPI workflows.
projects.description: OpenAPI Schema Parser helps integrate and process OpenAPI schemas within AsyncAPI-based tooling and workflows, improving interoperability and developer experience across API ecosystems.

projects.name: AsyncAPI Studio
projects.short_description: Visual editor and playground for AsyncAPI specifications.
projects.description: AsyncAPI Studio provides an interactive environment for creating, editing, validating, and previewing AsyncAPI documents, helping developers work more efficiently with event-driven API specifications.

form.question: How to support
form.response:
You can support AsyncAPI (part of Linux Foundation) by contributing to repositories, helping with community discussions, and supporting the initiative financially through Open Collective. Community support in the form of mentorship, onboarding help, and contributor guidance also plays an important role in sustaining the ecosystem.

More details on sponsorship: https://opencollective.com/asyncapi#category-CONTRIBUTE

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: AsyncAPI — part of the Linux Foundation — is an open source initiative focused on building specifications, tooling, and documentation for event-driven architectures. It helps developers design, document, and manage asynchronous APIs and event-driven systems consistently across different technologies and platforms.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Sustainable open source is less about doing everything yourself and more about building processes, trust, and contributor ownership early.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: Open source gave me opportunities to learn, collaborate, and grow alongside people from around the world. Maintaining projects is my way of giving back while helping create better developer experiences and healthier contributor communities.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: There is no such file I want to delete. If there were, it would already be gone rather than waiting around. Instead of deleting, I would say we need to keep improving our documentation. For example, a few docs still have broken links after a recent restructuring, and we are actively working on them.

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: Realizing contributor coordination sometimes takes more time than solving the problem yourself

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
